div.p: Colors: Header=Purple · Hot Entry=Blue · Warm Entry=Blue(dim) · Cold=Green · Padding=Gray

g.text: syscall_stats_t layout (24 bytes per entry, 3 fields × uint64_t)

div.p: L1 Cache (32 KB typical) 9,600 bytes = 30% of L1 capacity Entire g_stats[] fits in L1 after warmup Access latency: ~4 cycles per load/store
L2 Cache (256 KB typical) g_stats[] is 3.75% of L2 Cold-start: first access fetches 64-byte cache line covering 2.67 entries

ol.li: Bounds check: nr < STATS_TABLE_SIZE → 1 cycle (branch predicted)
total_ns += elapsed_ns → 1 L1 load + 1 store = ~4 cycles Total: ~15 cycles ≈ 5 ns at 3 GHz (L1-hot) vs. preceding ptrace context switch: ~3,000–10,000 cycles

div.p: Working set for ls /tmp: read(0), write(1), open(2), close(3), mmap(9), brk(12), openat(257), getdents64(217) → 8 entries × 24 bytes = 192 bytes = 3 cache lines All 3 cache lines L1-resident after first access

g.text: 5 entries × 24 bytes = 120 bytes ■ WARM — accessed on most programs
90 entries × 24 bytes = 2,160 bytes ■ WARM for common programs
157 entries × 24 bytes = 3,768 bytes □ COOL — occasional access
142 entries × 24 bytes = 3,408 bytes □ COLD — rarely touched
fits entirely in L1 after warmup
